calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-28-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey on her own behalf. Teresa Green for Leo Bautista (Petitioner).

The Court stated that notice was not required for the Victim Witness Program. The Court also dispensed with notice to Phillip Sims. Both Counsel do not object to George Sims' correspondence being part of the Court's record. Counsel addressed the issue of Pamela Sims statutory fees. The accounting was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Aug-02-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Pamela Sims. Teresa Green for and with Todd Christensen, for Leo Bautista and for Mark Piccolotti.

The Court suggested that the accounting be allowed, that it defer ruling on the statutory fees and commissions until final distribution, that Ms. Ebey be reimbursed $1,457.00 for costs, that all extraordinary fees for preparation of the accounting be deducted from Ms. Sims' shares, that it defer ruling on the balance of extraordinary fees to Ms. Ebey and Ms. Sims request for reimbursement of costs until final distribution and that the bond premium of $1,760.00 be reimbursed at this time. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel (subject to review of the order). The accounting petition was taken under submission to address the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Teresa Green for and with Leo Bautista.

The sale was confirmed at $220,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $220,000.00 is required.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Continued To Jun-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for Pam Sims. Teresa Green for Leo Bautista, Mark Piccolotti and Todd Christensen.

Ms. Ebey stated that she had communication with the Client by e mail regarding revision/redraft of the accounting, that there was no response and suggested the Court allow filing of an Attorney accounting to be presented to Ms. Green. Ms. Green withdrew the surcharge issue. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel was continued to June 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be set with a hearing date of June 29, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-23-2011 Continued To Apr-13-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Teresa Green for Mark Piccollotti, Todd Christenson and Leo Bautista. Amanda Ebey for Pamela Sims.

Ms. Ebey stated that her Client provided her with copies of the statements and a list of funds the Client contributed to the Estate from her own pocket and that she prepared a draft and sent it to her Client. In addition, Ms. Ebey stated that the Client e mailed her stating that some things were missing, that she e mailed her back requesting further information and that she had no communication with her Client within the last few days. Ms. Green raised the issue of sanctions, stated that it should be the responsibility of Ms. Sims rather than the Estate to prove that there should not be a surcharge against her fees as Personal Representative or any distributive share that she might receive. The matter was continued to April 13, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Pamela Simms. Teresa Green for and with Todd Christenson, for Leo Bautista and for Mark Piccolotti.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court granted the petition removing Pamela Simms and the petition to appoint Mr. Bautista as Administrator of the Estate, but the issue of bond and serving with limited authority was taken under submission. The issue of surcharge against Ms. Simms was deferred and continued to March 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel was also continued to March 23, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Green is to prepare the order removing Ms. Sims and present it to Ms. Ebey for approval as to form.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Off Calendar Dec-22-2010 Continued To Jan-26-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey for and with Pamela Sims. Teresa Green for and with Leo Bautista and for Mark Piccolotti.

Ms. Ebey requested that the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel be continued. Ms. Sims explained why the bond premium had not been paid, stated that she was dealing with family health issues and that that the Family decided that they wanted to keep the home so that the disabled Brother could reside in the home at some point. In addition, Ms. Sims requested a continuance and committed to paying the bond premium by the end of January. Ms. Green presented her objections and expresssed her concerns. All matters were continued to January 26, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court stated that the bond premium needs to be paid then.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Remove Administator And For Surcharge.
Off Calendar Dec-07-2010 Continued To Dec-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amanda Ebey, Esq. for Petitioner Pamela Sims. Teresa Green, Esq. for Petitioners Mark Piccolotti and Leo Bautista.

Ms. Ebey stated to the court that her client is ill and Ms Ebey had stopped her going on BART from Richmond this morning, since the property was damage by fire well before the decedent died, it has been sitting there uninhabitable and is not in a great condition to be sold and $11,000.00 was used, no cash left, the family is struggle with the loan and to pay bills. Her client has spoke with four Realtors and none of them think the house is worth more than a hundred thousand dollars and will be re appraised by probate referee and file request to reduce bond. Ms. Sims would actually like to have a continuance so she could address the Court and explain the progress that she had made; she's looking to close the estate. The Court inquired if it wouldn't be easier for someone else to take over to handle it and that she is willing to relinquish all these responsibilities. After hearing from counsel, the court continues all three petitions to Dec. 22, 2010 at 9:00 am in Dept.204. Bond premium to be posted by then.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.
